success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy was feasible (import_feasibility=0.92, low intrusiveness risk), enabling rapid MCP wrapping.
Repository structure and dependencies were detected reliably via zip fallback and AST scan, yielding broad function/class discovery.
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio transport with no runtime errors reported.
failure_reasons: Quality-gate weakness: original project tests are marked passed=false despite reported 100% coverage metadata, indicating test execution/collection inconsistency.
DeepWiki analysis failed (non-blocking), reducing external semantic validation depth.
Generation telemetry appears incomplete (tool_endpoints=0, generated LOC/size=0) and conflicts with discovered endpoint list, suggesting instrumentation/reporting defects.

node_performance.download_time: Completed within total runtime; exact per-node timing unavailable. Zip fallback succeeded, indicating resilient fetch/ingestion path.
node_performance.analysis_time: Likely moderate-to-high share of runtime due to AST scan across medium-size codebase (~47 files, several large modules).
node_performance.generation_time: Likely efficient given successful file creation and adapter scaffolding, but missing LOC/size metrics limit precision.
node_performance.test_time: MCP health test passed quickly; original project test phase appears not meaningfully executed (0 execution_time), so validation depth is limited.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Unknown from telemetry (reported 0 MB). No evidence of memory pressure, but metrics are not trustworthy.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Unknown from telemetry (reported 0%). Runtime (~221s) suggests non-trivial compute in analysis/generation.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low-to-moderate expected; only a small MCP output scaffold created. Reported generated size=0 indicates measurement gap.
